title: Ozi Work Queue — automated overnight lug dispatch
what: Ozi is the automated overnight work queue that dispatches implementation lugs to Claude agents without human involvement. Each nightly run scores all open lugs by ROI (impact/effort), respects urgency tiers and blocked_by constraints, routes each lug to the appropriate model tier (haiku/sonnet/opus based on model_fit), and records execution results.
why: Without Ozi, humans must manually review and dispatch every lug. Ozi enables continuous spoke improvement during idle hours - high-ROI haiku-tier work executes automatically while the user sleeps.

use_cases.title: Overnight haiku batch execution
use_cases.trigger: User marks impl lugs with model_fit=haiku and status=open
use_cases.outcome: Ozi dispatches all eligible lugs at next nightly run, each completes or records failure

use_cases.title: Blocked lug skip
use_cases.trigger: Lug has non-empty blocked_by referencing an unresolved lug
use_cases.outcome: Ozi skips the lug for this run - re-evaluated next night after blocker resolves

use_cases.title: ROI-based ordering
use_cases.outcome: Ozi dispatches by urgency tier first, then ROI descending within each tier

patterns.name: Haiku-first automation
patterns.description: Model lugs as haiku-executable when possible so Ozi can dispatch without human approval

patterns.name: ROI-gated dispatch
patterns.description: Set effort score carefully - it directly affects when a lug gets dispatched relative to peers

how.trigger: Nightly cron schedule (Minder-owned) or explicit wakeup with gardener mode
steps_summary: 1. Score all open lugs: roi = impact / effort, sort by urgency tier then roi
2. Filter: skip lugs with non-empty blocked_by referencing unresolved IDs
3. For each eligible lug in order: route to model per model_fit field
4. Execute the lug (agent reads perceive/execute/verify fields)
5. On success: move lug to completed/, record completion_evidence
6. On failure: record error, leave in open/ or in_progress/, increment retry count
constraints: model_fit must be set (haiku/sonnet/opus) for Ozi to dispatch
blocked_by references must resolve to empty or all-completed for dispatch eligibility
Ozi never modifies lugs it cannot complete - no partial execution

outputs: session-summary lug for each dispatched lug
state_changes: Lug status: open to in_progress to completed (or stays open on failure)

constraints: Ozi is Minder-owned - Framework never runs or simulates Tender/Minder manually
model_fit=haiku is required for auto-dispatch; sonnet/opus require explicit approval

open_questions: What retry limit before a lug is parked?

body: Ozi is the overnight automated work queue that dispatches implementation lugs to Claude agents without human involvement. Each nightly run is Minder-owned and cron-scheduled. The dispatch sequence is: score all open lugs by ROI (impact / effort), sort by urgency tier ascending then ROI descending within each tier, evaluate blocked_by and execute_when gates, then route each eligible lug to the appropriate model tier per its model_fit field. model_fit=haiku enables automatic dispatch; model_fit=sonnet or opus requires explicit user approval before Ozi will dispatch. Lugs are loaded from bytype/*/open/ and bytype/*/in_progress/. Blocked lugs (blocked_by referencing unresolved IDs, or execute_when.all_completed not satisfied) are skipped for the current run and re-evaluated the next night. On success, the lug moves from open/ to completed/ and a session-summary lug is created. On failure, the lug stays in open/ or in_progress/ with an incremented retry counter and an error note appended to execution_notes. Ozi never partially executes a lug — if the perceive/execute/verify cycle cannot be completed, it records the failure and moves on. The Interactive Work Queue Monitor (wai-ozi-work-queue-monitor skill) extends Ozi to monitor in real time during user sessions: it watches for new lugs, detects status changes, identifies stale work (in_progress >4h with no activity), and processes incoming teachings. Auto-dispatch only activates when the session has /wai-auto-on enabled. The work queue display at wakeup shows top-3 ready items by ROI with [W]ork / [A]uto-chain / [P]arallel dispatch options.
done_when: A lug with model_fit=haiku, non-empty perceive/execute/verify, and empty blocked_by is dispatched automatically on the next nightly run without user input
A lug with non-empty blocked_by where the referenced ID is still in open/ is skipped by Ozi and its skip is recorded in the run report
After a successful lug execution, the lug JSON is in bytype/*/completed/ and a session-summary lug exists for the same run
The wakeup work queue display shows urgency=1 lugs before any urgency=2 lugs regardless of their relative ROI scores
A lug with model_fit=sonnet does not dispatch automatically and appears in the wakeup queue with a 'requires approval' annotation
